title: Performance, Load, Supply-Chain Gates, and Production Client Defense · Automation and Data Client Systems · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A compliance analyst reconstructing a partial ingestion handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for performance, load, supply-chain gates, and production client defense, producing production HTTP client release dossier. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers production HTTP client release dossier after a delayed changed case. Learners progress th…
objectives: Measure latency distributions, queue time, throughput, error rate, and saturation against an end-to-end service objective. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Profile allocations, buffering, decoding, connection reuse, and response retention with representative sizes. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Test concurrency, fairness, quotas, backpressure, retry amplification, and graceful overload under changed demand. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Latency Throughput Budget · changed case · Review Latency Throughput Budget
steps.instruction:
review case — compliance handoff automation release P-4 doubles traffic and body size while p95 latency, memory, retries, and queue depth rise. Measure the bottleneck, preserve behavior, verify supply-chain gates and rollback, then defend production readiness.
